advantages: Fastest feedback loop — full suite runs in seconds.
Localize failures: a failing unit test points straight at the broken function.
Doubles as executable documentation of intended behaviour.
Catch regressions before they reach review or production.
disadvantages: Don't catch integration / wiring bugs (that's integration tests' job).
Mock-heavy tests can pass while real code is broken (over-isolated).
Writing tests for trivial code is wasted effort that creates maintenance burden.
False sense of security: high coverage with weak asserts is theatre.
